name: ETH Trend Reset Continuation v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow EMA-touch logic. The edge should come from a broader reset-and-reclaim structure that still fits a weak transition backdrop.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reset-and-reclaim entries remain too sparse, if deeper pullbacks mostly become full reversals, or if continuation is too weak after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional swing pulls back deeply enough to reset positioning, then regains fast value and resumes in the original direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Weak transition-up conditions often punish shallow continuation entries but still reward deeper resets that clear weak positioning before the next leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the cleaner lane for this because pullback and reclaim structure is usually less chaotic than on SOL while still expressing the same mechanism.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains fully range-bound, deeper resets may only bounce back to value and fail to continue.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but the back half underperforms, keep the reset logic and simplify exits before changing the entry.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reset depth, reclaim, and participation are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by a support zone around EMA_55 instead of crash-only reset rules

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
